title: Carlina fennica
intro: Carlina fennica, also known as the Arctic carline thistle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as boosting the immune system. The plant's bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones and polyphenols, are responsible for its medicinal properties. Carlina fennica is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its health-promoting effects.
